title: Spring Data JPA Deep Dive
summary: N+1 problem, fetch strategies, JPQL vs Criteria API, projections, specifications, and JPA performance tuning.

sections.title: N+1 Query Problem
sections.content:
sequenceDiagram
    participant App
    participant DB
    App->>DB: SELECT * FROM author (1 query)
    DB-->>App: [Author1, Author2, Author3...AuthorN]
    App->>DB: SELECT * FROM book WHERE author_id=1
    App->>DB: SELECT * FROM book WHERE author_id=2
    App->>DB: SELECT * FROM book WHERE author_id=3
    Note over App,DB: N more queries!
    App->>DB: SELECT * FROM book WHERE author_id=N
    Note over DB: Total: 1 + N queries

sections.title: Fetch Strategy Comparison
sections.content:
graph LR
    subgraph LAZY default
        L1[Load Authors] -->|N separate queries| L2[Load Books one-by-one]
    end
    subgraph JOIN FETCH
        J1[One JOIN query] --> J2[All data loaded]
    end
    subgraph BATCH SIZE 50
        B1[Load Authors] --> B2[Load Books in batches of 50]
    end
    style J2 fill:#059669,color:#fff
    style L2 fill:#dc2626,color:#fff

sections.title: Enable Hibernate SQL logging to spot N+1
sections.content: Add spring.jpa.show-sql=true and spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.format_sql=true to application.properties. If you see the same query repeated many times with different IDs, you have an N+1 problem.

sections.question: What is the most efficient way to fix N+1 when loading authors with their books for a report?
options: Switch all @OneToMany to EAGER fetch type
Use JOIN FETCH in a JPQL query or @EntityGraph to load both in a single query
Load authors and books in separate queries and join in Java
Use @Transactional to keep the session open longer
sections.explanation: JOIN FETCH or @EntityGraph loads all the data in a single SQL JOIN query, eliminating N+1. Switching to EAGER globally causes N+1 in other places and is worse. Joining in Java is inefficient. @Transactional with open session just enables lazy loading — it doesn't prevent N additional queries.
